title: 4 Bedroom Houses for Rent on Cleveland's West Side
metaTitle: 4 Bedroom Houses for Rent Cleveland West Side
metaDescription: Searching for a 4 bedroom house for rent on Cleveland's west side? See current rent ranges, transit near Ohio City, and Section 8-friendly options here.
primaryKeyword: 4 bedroom houses for rent cleveland west side
secondaryKeywords: large house for rent west side cleveland
answerBox: 4-bedroom houses are the least common size in Cleveland's rental market, and our west-side inventory (Ohio City, Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway, Old Brooklyn) is smaller than our east-side portfolio. When available, 4-bedroom homes typically rent from about $1,200 to $1,800 a month, and every home we manage accepts Section 8 vouchers.

sections.h2: 4-bedroom houses for rent on Cleveland's west side
body: Four-bedroom homes are the least common size across our entire portfolio — most of what we manage is 2- and 3-bedroom — and our west-side footprint itself is smaller than our east-side one. On the west side, our homes are concentrated in **ZIP codes 44102 (Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway), 44109 (Old Brooklyn/Brooklyn Centre), and 44113 (Ohio City/Tremont)**, so a 4-bedroom house in one of those specific ZIPs won't always be sitting open.

If a larger household needs four bedrooms on the west side, the fastest path is to [book a showing](/p/book-showing) or [contact our team](/p/book-showing) directly with your size and budget — we'll tell you honestly what's currently available rather than list a unit that may already be gone.

sections.h2: How much does a 4-bedroom house rent for on the west side?
body: Across our current Cleveland-wide availability, 4-bedroom homes run **$1,200 to $1,800 a month**, averaging around $1,510. There is no separate published west-side-only benchmark, but for general market context, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report lists Cleveland's overall median asking rent (all bedroom sizes) at $1,250, while RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report — covering larger professionally-managed apartment complexes — shows 3-bedroom units alone averaging $2,716, with 4-bedroom units rarer still in that segment of the market.

rows: Median asking rent, all Cleveland listings

body: Ohio City is served by the RTA **Red Line** at the Ohio City station, one stop from Downtown's Tower City hub, and is home to the historic West Side Market at W. 25th Street and Lorain Avenue. Detroit-Shoreway, just northwest, contains the Gordon Square Arts District, and Edgewater Park sits on the Lake Erie shoreline nearby. Old Brooklyn, to the south, is near the Cleveland Metroparks Zoo and is generally a longer commute to University Circle or the east side than Ohio City is.

For a larger household, proximity to I-90 (lakefront) or I-71 (southwest) matters more for commute time than exact neighborhood, since most west-side ZIPs reach Downtown in well under 15 minutes by car.

items: **Rail:** RTA Red Line, Ohio City station one stop from Tower City
**Landmarks:** West Side Market (Ohio City), Gordon Square Arts District (Detroit-Shoreway), Edgewater Park, Cleveland Metroparks Zoo (Old Brooklyn)
**Highways:** I-90 along the lakefront, I-71 to the southwest

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers for larger west-side homes
body: Every home we manage, regardless of bedroom count or side of town, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is kept HUD-inspection-ready. CMHA administers the voucher program for Cuyahoga County and accepts [preliminary applications online](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) year-round; larger households should confirm their voucher bedroom-size allowance and current payment standard directly with CMHA before touring, since those figures are set and updated by the housing authority, not by individual landlords.

As elsewhere in Cleveland, the city has no source-of-income ordinance mandating voucher acceptance — only a handful of suburbs (Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale) currently have that protection. See our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for more on how vouchers work locally.

sections.h2: What a 4-bedroom west-side rental typically includes
body: Four-bedroom homes on the west side tend to be older single-family houses with a full basement, most often in Old Brooklyn or the Clark-Fulton-adjacent blocks of Ohio City and Tremont, where lot sizes are somewhat larger than the near-Downtown blocks. If a 4-bedroom isn't available when you inquire, a **3-bedroom** ([west side listings](/cleveland-rentals/houses/3-bedroom-houses-for-rent-cleveland-west-side/)) may fit depending on room configuration — or browse our full [Cleveland houses for rent](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) to compare sizes and areas side by side.

faq.q: Are 4-bedroom houses for rent common on Cleveland's west side?
faq.a: No — 4-bedroom homes are the least common size across our entire portfolio, and our west-side footprint (mainly ZIPs 44102, 44109, and 44113) is smaller than our east-side one. Availability is inconsistent; contact us directly to check what's currently open.

faq.q: How much does a 4-bedroom house rent for in Cleveland?
faq.a: Our current Cleveland-wide 4-bedroom range runs about $1,200 to $1,800 a month, averaging around $1,510. There's no separate west-side-only benchmark published, so this reflects our portfolio overall rather than west-side units specifically.

faq.q: What west-side neighborhoods might have a 4-bedroom rental available?
faq.a: Our west-side inventory is concentrated in Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (44102), Old Brooklyn/Brooklyn Centre (44109), and Ohio City/Tremont (44113). Larger homes are somewhat more likely in Old Brooklyn, where lot sizes tend to be larger, but availability changes frequently.

faq.q: Do 4-bedroom west-side rentals accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Yes. Every home we manage, including larger west-side units, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Confirm your voucher's bedroom-size allowance and payment standard with CMHA before touring, since those are set by the housing authority.

faq.q: What if a 4-bedroom house isn't available on the west side right now?
faq.a: Ask about a 3-bedroom in the same area, or expand your search to include our larger east-side portfolio, where more of our homes are located overall. Contact our team with your size and budget and we'll tell you honestly what's currently open.
